heading: Missing persons: initial report
label: 1502 Missing persons: initial report
notes: Amendments1997—Subsecs. (b), (c). added subsec. (b) and redesignated former subsec. (b) as (c).Pub. L. 105–851996—Subsec. (a)(2). , substituted “10 days” for “48 hours” and “Secretary concerned” for “theater component commander with jurisdiction over the missing person”.Pub. L. 104–201, § 578(b)(1)(A)Subsec. (b). , struck out at end “The theater component commander through whom the report with respect to the missing person is transmitted under subsection (b) shall ensure that all pertinent information relating to the whereabouts and status of the missing person that results from the preliminary assessment or from actions taken to locate the person is properly safeguarded to avoid loss, damage, or modification.”Pub. L. 104–201, § 578(b)(1)(D), (C), redesignated subsec. (c) as (b) and struck out former subsec. (b) which read as follows: “.—Upon reviewing a report under subsection (a) recommending that a person be placed in a missing status, the theater component commander shall ensure that all necessary actions are being taken, and all appropriate assets are being used, to resolve the status of the missing person. Not later than 14 days after receiving the report, the theater component commander shall forward the report to the Secretary of Defense or the Secretary concerned in accordance with procedures prescribed under . The theater component commander shall include with such report a certification that all necessary actions are being taken, and all appropriate assets are being used, to resolve the status of the missing person.”Pub. L. 104–201, § 578(b)(1)(B)section 1501(b) of this titleTransmission Through Theater Component CommanderSubsec. (c). , redesignated subsec. (c) as (b).Pub. L. 104–201, § 578(b)(1)(C)

children.heading: .—Preliminary Assessment and Recommendation by Commander
children.label: a .—Preliminary Assessment and Recommendation by Commander

children.text: recommend that the person be placed in a missing status; and

not later than 10 days after receiving such information, transmit a report containing that recommendation to the Secretary concerned in accordance with procedures prescribed under .section 1501(b) of this title

children.heading: .—Transmission of Advisory Copy to Theater Component Commander
children.label: b .—Transmission of Advisory Copy to Theater Component Commander
children.text: When transmitting a report under subsection (a)(2) recommending that a person be placed in a missing status, the commander transmitting that report shall transmit an advisory copy of the report to the theater component commander with jurisdiction over the missing person.

children.heading: .—Safeguarding and Forwarding of Records
children.label: c .—Safeguarding and Forwarding of Records
children.text: A commander making a preliminary assessment under subsection (a) with respect to a missing person shall (in accordance with procedures prescribed under ) safeguard and forward for official use any information relating to the whereabouts and status of the missing person that results from the preliminary assessment or from actions taken to locate the person.section 1501 of this title
